Assignment 02: Prompt Exercise

EPPS 6323 – Knowledge Mining

Assignment 02

This assignment involves designing and refining prompts to conduct a structured systematic literature review on Data Mining (DM) and Machine Learning (ML) using multiple AI models.

1. Objective

The objective of this exercise is to examine how different large language models (LLMs)—including ChatGPT, Copilot, and Grok 3—can support academic research tasks, particularly the generation of systematic literature reviews.

This study focuses on how prompt refinement improves the precision, structure, and analytical depth of AI-generated literature reviews. By iteratively modifying prompts and comparing model outputs, the exercise evaluates how prompt engineering can enhance the quality of scholarly synthesis.

2. Step 1: Initial Prompt Creation

The first step involved developing a baseline prompt designed to generate a structured systematic literature review of approximately 2,000 words. The prompt aimed to instruct the AI models to synthesize existing research while maintaining an academic tone and a clear structure.

Baseline Prompt

Conduct a 2,000-word structured systematic literature review on the applications of data mining and machine learning in real-world domains. Include a methodology section, synthesize key findings, identify trends and research gaps, and propose one testable hypothesis. Use an academic tone and emulate systematic review standards.

This baseline prompt served as the starting point for evaluating the strengths and weaknesses of each AI model’s response.

3. Step 2: Analysis of Model Responses

The responses generated by each model were evaluated based on three criteria:

  • Structural organization of the literature review
  • Quality of synthesis and analytical depth
  • Accuracy and relevance of references or citations

ChatGPT (OpenAI)

ChatGPT demonstrated strong capabilities in synthesizing broad research themes and generating coherent academic prose. However, the responses occasionally required more specific instructions to clearly outline methodological procedures and to maintain strict systematic review conventions.

Copilot (Microsoft)

Copilot provided well-structured responses and was particularly effective in generating concise summaries and referencing external sources. Its outputs tended to emphasize clarity and organization but sometimes lacked deeper analytical synthesis.

Grok 3 (xAI)

Grok 3 offered more exploratory insights and generated creative interpretations of emerging trends. Its responses often included novel hypotheses and forward-looking perspectives, though additional prompt refinement was necessary to ensure methodological clarity.

4. Step 3: Prompt Refinement

To improve the quality of the responses, the original prompt was refined to include a specific persona and clearer domain constraints. Introducing a professional persona can guide AI systems toward more specialized reasoning and structured academic output.

Refined Prompt

Imagine you are a data scientist conducting a 2,000-word systematic literature review on how data mining and machine learning are applied in domains such as healthcare, finance, and education. Outline a clear methodology, synthesize key findings with original insights, identify major trends and research gaps, and propose one testable hypothesis. Maintain a rigorous academic tone throughout the review.

The refined prompt provides clearer guidance regarding expertise, disciplinary context, and expected analytical depth.

5. Step 4: Cross-Model Collaboration

In the final stage, the strongest elements from the responses generated by ChatGPT, Copilot, and Grok 3 were synthesized into a single cohesive literature review. This approach demonstrates how outputs from multiple AI systems can be integrated to produce a more comprehensive scholarly narrative.

Synthesized Literature Review: Applications of Data Mining and Machine Learning

Methodology

The literature review follows structured search principles inspired by systematic review practices. Academic databases—including IEEE Xplore, ACM Digital Library, and PubMed—were searched using combinations of the following keywords:

  • “Data Mining”
  • “Machine Learning”
  • “Healthcare”
  • “Finance”
  • “Education”

The search focused primarily on publications from 2018 to 2024, emphasizing studies that demonstrate practical applications of machine learning and data mining techniques in real-world operational contexts.

Synthesis of Key Findings

Healthcare

In healthcare, machine learning technologies have evolved from diagnostic support tools to predictive and preventative systems. Deep learning algorithms are increasingly used in medical imaging, oncology diagnostics, and cardiovascular risk prediction. In addition, hospital administrators employ data mining techniques to improve operational efficiency, including patient flow optimization and resource allocation.

Finance

In the financial sector, machine learning applications are widely used in algorithmic trading, fraud detection, and credit risk assessment. Algorithms such as Random Forests and Support Vector Machines are commonly applied to detect anomalies in financial transactions and predict borrower risk profiles. These techniques often outperform traditional statistical models in identifying complex nonlinear patterns.

Education

Within education, the emerging field of Educational Data Mining (EDM) focuses on analyzing student performance data to support personalized learning systems. Machine learning algorithms are used to predict dropout risks, evaluate student engagement patterns, and optimize adaptive learning platforms.

Emerging Trends and Research Gaps

A significant trend across domains is the growing demand for Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). As machine learning models are increasingly deployed in critical sectors, stakeholders are demanding greater transparency and interpretability in automated decision-making systems.

Despite technological advances, several important research gaps remain:

  • Limited transparency in complex neural network models
  • Ethical concerns related to algorithmic bias
  • Unequal access to high-quality data in low-resource settings
  • Governance challenges associated with automated decision systems

Addressing these challenges is essential for ensuring responsible AI adoption across public and private sectors.

Proposed Research Hypothesis

H1:
The adoption of Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) frameworks in organizational decision-making systems will significantly increase stakeholder trust compared to traditional black-box machine learning models.

Conclusion

The application of data mining and machine learning across healthcare, finance, and education demonstrates a broader transformation toward data-driven decision-making. While advances in predictive analytics and automated systems offer substantial efficiency gains, their long-term success depends on transparency, ethical governance, and responsible deployment.

6. Reflection

This exercise highlights the importance of prompt engineering in improving AI-assisted research workflows. The transition from the baseline prompt to the refined prompt illustrates how clearer instructions, domain constraints, and professional personas can significantly enhance the quality of AI-generated academic writing.

Three key insights emerged from the experiment:

  • Prompt specificity improves analytical depth.
  • Different AI models demonstrate complementary strengths.
  • Human oversight remains essential to verify sources and ensure academic integrity.

These findings suggest that AI tools can effectively support scholarly research when used as collaborative assistants rather than fully autonomous authors.

References

Bozeman, B. (2007). Public Value and Public Interest. Georgetown University Press.

Dixon, M. F., Halperin, I., & Bilokon, P. (2020). Machine Learning in Finance: From Theory to Practice. Springer.

Fayyad, U., Piatetsky-Shapiro, G., & Smyth, P. (1996). From data mining to knowledge discovery in databases. AI Magazine, 17(3), 37–54.

Topol, E. J. (2019). High-performance medicine: The convergence of human and artificial intelligence. Nature Medicine, 25(1), 44–56.

White, J., et al. (2023). A prompt pattern catalog to enhance prompting with ChatGPT. arXiv preprint arXiv:2302.11382.
